body.cutover_rule: This claim applies only to new post-cutover permit.revoked events. Pre-cutover revoked status is a legacy revocation status snapshot and is not revocation evidence for this claim.

canonical_payload.signature_exclusion: The signature field is excluded before canonicalization.
canonical_payload.identity_binding: permit_id and project_id are both signed to prevent cross-project replay.
canonical_payload.actor_rule: actor_id is an opaque UUID. actor_kind is one of user, service_account, system, or api_key. The payload carries no email, name, or display field.
canonical_payload.reason_rule: reason_code is a taxonomy code, not free text.
canonical_payload.temporal_rule: revoked_at and effective_at are RFC 3339 timestamps. In v1, effective_at MUST equal revoked_at.

signature.key_resolution: Verify against a permit_binding_signing key from the supplied trust root active at revoked_at. If multiple active keys are supplied and no key id is carried by the event payload, any active trusted key may support the signature.

verdicts.supported: strict event payload is present, effective_at equals revoked_at, permit_id and project_id match the requested scope, and signature verifies under a trusted permit-binding key.
verdicts.disproved: signature verification fails, project_id does not match the requested scope, effective_at differs from revoked_at, or actor identity violates the opaque UUID rule.
verdicts.insufficient_evidence: required payload, signature, trust-root evidence, or referenced permit/project identity is missing.
verdicts.unverifiable_scope: canonicalization profile, trust-root profile, or actor_kind taxonomy is unsupported.
